fields.title: Open Access Publishing: A New Era for Research
fields.title_en: Open Access Publishing: A New Era for Research
fields.summary: Open access publishing is transforming how scientific research reaches the public, removing paywalls and enabling researchers worldwide to share their findings freely.
fields.summary_en: Open access publishing is transforming how scientific research reaches the public, removing paywalls and enabling researchers worldwide to share their findings freely.
fields.body:
The movement towards open access publishing has gained significant momentum over the past decade. Institutions and funders are increasingly mandating that research outputs be made freely available, recognising that knowledge should be a public good.

For journals operating under traditional subscription models, this represents both a challenge and an opportunity. The transition requires new funding mechanisms, but ultimately serves the broader scientific community.
fields.body_en:
The movement towards open access publishing has gained significant momentum over the past decade. Institutions and funders are increasingly mandating that research outputs be made freely available, recognising that knowledge should be a public good.

For journals operating under traditional subscription models, this represents both a challenge and an opportunity. The transition requires new funding mechanisms, but ultimately serves the broader scientific community.
fields.meta_description: Discover how open access publishing is reshaping academic research and what it means for journals and researchers worldwide.
fields.meta_description_en: Discover how open access publishing is reshaping academic research and what it means for journals and researchers worldwide.

fields.title: Peer Review Under the Microscope
fields.summary: As the volume of submitted manuscripts grows, journals are rethinking how peer review works — and whether the traditional model remains fit for purpose.
fields.summary_en: As the volume of submitted manuscripts grows, journals are rethinking how peer review works — and whether the traditional model remains fit for purpose.
fields.body:
Peer review has long been considered the gold standard of academic quality control. Yet it is not without its critics. Reviewers are unpaid volunteers, timelines stretch for months, and bias — conscious or otherwise — can creep into the process.

Several journals are experimenting with open peer review, where reviewer identities are disclosed, or post-publication review, which shifts scrutiny to after a paper is published. Each model carries trade-offs that the community continues to debate.
fields.body_en:
Peer review has long been considered the gold standard of academic quality control. Yet it is not without its critics. Reviewers are unpaid volunteers, timelines stretch for months, and bias — conscious or otherwise — can creep into the process.

Several journals are experimenting with open peer review, where reviewer identities are disclosed, or post-publication review, which shifts scrutiny to after a paper is published. Each model carries trade-offs that the community continues to debate.
fields.meta_description: Exploring the challenges and innovations reshaping peer review in academic publishing.
fields.meta_description_en: Exploring the challenges and innovations reshaping peer review in academic publishing.

fields.title: Funding the Future: Our Crowdfunding Milestone
fields.title_en: Funding the Future: Our Crowdfunding Milestone
fields.summary: We are delighted to announce that our community fundraising campaign has reached 60% of its target, a testament to the passion researchers and readers share for independent publishing.
fields.summary_en: We are delighted to announce that our community fundraising campaign has reached 60% of its target, a testament to the passion researchers and readers share for independent publishing.
fields.body:
When we launched our crowdfunding campaign last autumn, we hoped to raise enough to sustain our infrastructure for three years. The response has exceeded all expectations.

Contributions have come from researchers at over 40 countries, independent readers, and a number of university libraries who recognise the value of a community-owned publishing platform. We are grateful to every single backer.

The remaining 40% will unlock expanded editorial capacity and translation support. If you haven't already, please consider joining the movement.
fields.body_en:
When we launched our crowdfunding campaign last autumn, we hoped to raise enough to sustain our infrastructure for three years. The response has exceeded all expectations.

Contributions have come from researchers at over 40 countries, independent readers, and a number of university libraries who recognise the value of a community-owned publishing platform. We are grateful to every single backer.

The remaining 40% will unlock expanded editorial capacity and translation support. If you haven't already, please consider joining the movement.
fields.meta_description: Our crowdfunding campaign has hit 60% — read the story behind the milestone and how you can help us reach the goal.
fields.meta_description_en: Our crowdfunding campaign has hit 60% — read the story behind the milestone and how you can help us reach the goal.

fields.title: Introducing Multilingual Abstracts Across All Journals
fields.title_en: Introducing Multilingual Abstracts Across All Journals
fields.summary: From this month, every paper published through our platform will include abstracts in English, French, German, and Spanish — making research more accessible to a global audience.
fields.summary_en: From this month, every paper published through our platform will include abstracts in English, French, German, and Spanish — making research more accessible to a global audience.
fields.body:
Language has always been a barrier in academic publishing. The vast majority of high-impact journals publish exclusively in English, effectively excluding researchers and readers whose first language is not English.

Our new multilingual abstract feature is a first step towards addressing this. Authors submitting to any journal on our platform can now provide translated abstracts, and we offer assisted translation for those who need it.

We believe this will open up our content to a significantly wider audience and encourage submissions from researchers in underrepresented regions.
fields.body_en:
Language has always been a barrier in academic publishing. The vast majority of high-impact journals publish exclusively in English, effectively excluding researchers and readers whose first language is not English.

Our new multilingual abstract feature is a first step towards addressing this. Authors submitting to any journal on our platform can now provide translated abstracts, and we offer assisted translation for those who need it.

We believe this will open up our content to a significantly wider audience and encourage submissions from researchers in underrepresented regions.
fields.meta_description: All journals on our platform now support multilingual abstracts in four languages, broadening access to research globally.
fields.meta_description_en: All journals on our platform now support multilingual abstracts in four languages, broadening access to research globally.

fields.title: Meet the Editorial Board: Spotlight on Dr Amara Osei
fields.title_en: Meet the Editorial Board: Spotlight on Dr Amara Osei
fields.summary: In the first of our editorial board spotlight series, we speak with Dr Amara Osei about her work in computational biology and her vision for open science on the African continent.
fields.summary_en: In the first of our editorial board spotlight series, we speak with Dr Amara Osei about her work in computational biology and her vision for open science on the African continent.
fields.body:
Dr Amara Osei joined our editorial board eighteen months ago, bringing with her a decade of experience in computational biology and a deep commitment to making science accessible.

What drew you to open access publishing?

"I grew up in Ghana and studied at the University of Accra. The papers I needed to read for my research were often locked behind paywalls I couldn't afford. When I eventually moved to a well-funded institution, I promised myself I would work to change that for the next generation of researchers."

What do you see as the biggest challenge facing open science today?

"Sustainability. The article processing charge model simply shifts the financial burden from readers to authors, which disadvantages researchers without institutional backing. We need genuinely community-funded models — which is exactly what this platform is building."
fields.body_en:
Dr Amara Osei joined our editorial board eighteen months ago, bringing with her a decade of experience in computational biology and a deep commitment to making science accessible.

What drew you to open access publishing?

"I grew up in Ghana and studied at the University of Accra. The papers I needed to read for my research were often locked behind paywalls I couldn't afford. When I eventually moved to a well-funded institution, I promised myself I would work to change that for the next generation of researchers."

What do you see as the biggest challenge facing open science today?

"Sustainability. The article processing charge model simply shifts the financial burden from readers to authors, which disadvantages researchers without institutional backing. We need genuinely community-funded models — which is exactly what this platform is building."
fields.meta_description: Dr Amara Osei discusses computational biology, open science in Africa, and the future of community-funded publishing.
fields.meta_description_en: Dr Amara Osei discusses computational biology, open science in Africa, and the future of community-funded publishing.

fields.title: New Partnership with the Global Libraries Network
fields.title_en: New Partnership with the Global Libraries Network
fields.summary: We have signed a formal partnership with the Global Libraries Network, giving their 2,400 member institutions seamless access to every journal on our platform.
fields.summary_en: We have signed a formal partnership with the Global Libraries Network, giving their 2,400 member institutions seamless access to every journal on our platform.
fields.body:
Libraries have always been at the heart of knowledge dissemination. The Global Libraries Network represents institutions across 80 countries, from national libraries in major capitals to small university libraries serving communities that might otherwise have no access to current research.

Under the terms of the partnership, member institutions will be able to offer their patrons full access to every journal hosted on our platform. In return, a portion of their membership fees will be directed back into our author support fund, helping researchers who cannot afford article processing charges.

We see this as a model for how the publishing and library communities can work together to sustain open access for the long term.
fields.body_en:
Libraries have always been at the heart of knowledge dissemination. The Global Libraries Network represents institutions across 80 countries, from national libraries in major capitals to small university libraries serving communities that might otherwise have no access to current research.

Under the terms of the partnership, member institutions will be able to offer their patrons full access to every journal hosted on our platform. In return, a portion of their membership fees will be directed back into our author support fund, helping researchers who cannot afford article processing charges.

We have always been at the heart of knowledge dissemination. The Global Libraries Network represents institutions across 80 countries, from national libraries in major capitals to small university libraries serving communities that might otherwise have no access to current research.
fields.meta_description: Our new partnership with the Global Libraries Network extends platform access to 2,400 institutions across 80 countries.
fields.meta_description_en: Our new partnership with the Global Libraries Network extends platform access to 2,400 institutions across 80 countries.

fields.title: Preprints and Trust: Lessons from the Pandemic
fields.title_en: Preprints and Trust: Lessons from the Pandemic
fields.summary: The COVID-19 pandemic accelerated the adoption of preprints but also exposed their risks. We examine what the scientific community has learnt — and what still needs to change.
fields.summary_en: The COVID-19 pandemic accelerated the adoption of preprints but also exposed their risks. We examine what the scientific community has learnt — and what still needs to change.
fields.body:
During the early months of the pandemic, preprints became the primary vehicle for sharing urgent research findings. Turnaround times that normally ran to months were compressed to days. In some cases this was invaluable. In others, it amplified misinformation before errors could be corrected.

The episode has prompted a serious reckoning within the community about how preprints should be communicated to non-specialist audiences, and what responsibilities platforms and journalists bear when amplifying unreviewed research.

We have since introduced a clear preprint badge on all papers that have not yet completed peer review, along with a plain-language explainer of what that means. It is a small step, but a necessary one.
fields.body_en:
During the early months of the pandemic, preprints became the primary vehicle for sharing urgent research findings. Turnaround times that normally ran to months were compressed to days. In some cases this was invaluable. In others, it amplified misinformation before errors could be corrected.

The episode has prompted a serious reckoning within the community about how preprints should be communicated to non-specialist audiences, and what responsibilities platforms and journalists bear when amplifying unreviewed research.

We have since introduced a clear preprint badge on all papers that have not yet completed peer review, along with a plain-language explainer of what that means. It is a small step, but a necessary one.
fields.meta_description: What did the pandemic teach us about preprints, trust, and communicating science responsibly?
fields.meta_description_en: What did the pandemic teach us about preprints, trust, and communicating science responsibly?

fields.title: Platform Update: Improved Search and Discovery
fields.title_en: Platform Update: Improved Search and Discovery
fields.summary: Our latest platform release brings a significantly improved search experience, with full-text search across abstracts, keyword filtering, and personalised recommendations.
fields.summary_en: Our latest platform release brings a significantly improved search experience, with full-text search across abstracts, keyword filtering, and personalised recommendations.
fields.body:
Finding relevant research should not be a frustrating experience. In user testing earlier this year, we identified that many readers struggled to surface papers relevant to their interests, particularly when searching across multiple journals.

The new search interface introduces full-text search across titles, abstracts, and author keywords. Users can now filter results by discipline, publication date, language, and open access status. We have also introduced a "similar papers" feature on individual article pages.

These changes are live now. We would love to hear your feedback — use the link at the bottom of any page to get in touch.
fields.body_en:
Finding relevant research should not be a frustrating experience. In user testing earlier this year, we identified that many readers struggled to surface papers relevant to their interests, particularly when searching across multiple journals.

The new search interface introduces full-text search across titles, abstracts, and author keywords. Users can now filter results by discipline, publication date, language, and open access status. We have also introduced a "similar papers" feature on individual article pages.

These changes are live now. We would love to hear your feedback — use the link at the bottom of any page to get in touch.
fields.meta_description: Full-text search, keyword filters, and personalised recommendations are now live across the platform.
fields.meta_description_en: Full-text search, keyword filters, and personalised recommendations are now live across the platform.

fields.title: Why We Chose a Co-operative Model
fields.title_en: Why We Chose a Co-operative Model
fields.summary: From the very beginning, we knew that a traditional company structure would not serve our mission. Here is the story of why we incorporated as a co-operative — and what that means for every member.
fields.summary_en: From the very beginning, we knew that a traditional company structure would not serve our mission. Here is the story of why we incorporated as a co-operative — and what that means for every member.
fields.body:
When the founding team sat down to think about governance, the options before us were stark. A private company could grow quickly but would ultimately answer to shareholders, not the community. A charity offered mission protection but limited democratic participation.

A co-operative felt different. In a co-operative, every member has a voice. Surplus is reinvested rather than extracted. The organisation exists to serve its members, not the other way around.

We are now three years in, and the co-operative structure has shaped every major decision we have made — from setting subscription fees to selecting which journals to host. It is slower than a traditional top-down structure, but it is also more robust. Our members trust us because they own us.
fields.body_en:
When the founding team sat down to think about governance, the options before us were stark. A private company could grow quickly but would ultimately answer to shareholders, not the community. A charity offered mission protection but limited democratic participation.

A co-operative felt different. In a co-operative, every member has a voice. Surplus is reinvested rather than extracted. The organisation exists to serve its members, not the other way around.

We are now three years in, and the co-operative structure has shaped every major decision we have made — from setting subscription fees to selecting which journals to host. It is slower than a traditional top-down structure, but it is also more robust. Our members trust us because they own us.
fields.meta_description: Why we structured ourselves as a co-operative, and how that decision shapes everything we do.
fields.meta_description_en: Why we structured ourselves as a co-operative, and how that decision shapes everything we do.

fields.title: Calls for Papers: Spring 2026 Issue
fields.summary: Six of our partner journals are now accepting submissions for their Spring 2026 issues. Deadlines range from mid-January to end of February — check the full list inside.
fields.summary_en: Six of our partner journals are now accepting submissions for their Spring 2026 issues. Deadlines range from mid-January to end of February — check the full list inside.
fields.body:
The following journals are currently open for submissions. Click each title to view the full call for papers, author guidelines, and submission portal.

  • Journal of Environmental Humanities — deadline 31 January 2026. Theme: Climate Grief and Collective Action.
  • Postcolonial Studies Quarterly — deadline 14 February 2026. Open submissions.
  • Computational Linguistics Review — deadline 28 February 2026. Special issue: Large Language Models and Linguistic Theory.
  • Community Health Journal — deadline 7 February 2026. Theme: Mental Health in Rural Communities.
  • Archives of Interdisciplinary Science — deadline 21 February 2026. Open submissions.
  • Education Policy Forum — deadline 28 February 2026. Theme: Equity in Higher Education Funding.

All submissions are free of charge for authors. For queries, contact the relevant editorial team via the journal page.
